patterns.reason: cpu_interpreter_exception; semantics tested, file parity not required

shape_exclude.reason: TS passes a Uint8Array byte view as one value; C++ receives the same ROM TOC byte span as pointer plus size at the native ABI boundary.
TS TOC payload exposes RomAsset format records; C++ stores the same decoded entries as RomSourceEntry records at the native source-stack boundary.
TS uses null for an absent project root; C++ stores the same absence with std::optional.

exclude.reason: TS exposes RawRomSource as a structural TypeScript input contract; C++ keeps the concrete RomSourceStack public API instead of adding a virtual facade.
TS exposes RawRomSource as a structural TypeScript input contract; C++ keeps the concrete RomSourceStack public API instead of adding a virtual facade.
TS exposes RawRomSource as a structural TypeScript input contract; C++ keeps the concrete RomSourceStack public API instead of adding a virtual facade.
TS exposes RawRomSource as a structural TypeScript input contract; C++ keeps the concrete RomSourceStack public API instead of adding a virtual facade.
TS exposes RawRomSource as a structural TypeScript input contract; C++ keeps the concrete RomSourceStack public API instead of adding a virtual facade.
TS exposes RawRomSource as a structural TypeScript input contract; C++ keeps the concrete RomSourceStack public API instead of adding a virtual facade.
C++ source.h owns these source-stack record carriers because they embed C++ RomAssetInfo; TS owns the same wire-format types in rompack/format.ts.
C++ source.h owns these source-stack record carriers because they embed C++ RomAssetInfo; TS owns the same wire-format types in rompack/format.ts.
C++ source.h owns these source-stack record carriers because they embed C++ RomAssetInfo; TS owns the same wire-format types in rompack/format.ts.

shape_exclude.reason: TS source layers carry payload bytes as Uint8Array; C++ stores the same layer payload bytes through the native vector owner pointer.
TS source stack methods expose RomAsset records; C++ exposes the same decoded payload access through RomSourceEntry/RomAssetInfo native records.
TS source stack methods expose RomAsset records; C++ exposes the same decoded payload view through RomSourceEntry/RomAssetInfo native records.
TS source stack returns RomAsset or null at the rompack format boundary; C++ returns optional RomSourceEntry at the native source-stack boundary.
TS source stack returns RomAsset or null at the rompack format boundary; C++ returns optional RomSourceEntry at the native source-stack boundary.
TS source stack lists RomAsset records and accepts optional asset_type; C++ lists RomSourceEntry records and accepts optional string_view at the native boundary.
TS linked images own the ProgramImage object directly; C++ owns the same linked image through unique_ptr at the native ownership boundary.
TS uses null for absent program metadata; C++ owns the same optional metadata through unique_ptr at the native ownership boundary.
TS passes nullable ProgramSymbolsImage references; C++ passes the same optional metadata as native pointers at the linker ABI boundary.
C++ Machine receives the native MicrotaskQueue owner explicitly; TS owns the same microtask runtime outside the Machine constructor in the host event loop.
TS stores string pool ids as number values; C++ stores the same ids as uint32_t words at the native ABI boundary.

exclude.reason: TS compiler emits object-image sections; C++ runtime consumes linked images and has no compiler producer surface.

shape_exclude.reason: TS passes a Uint8Array byte view as one value; C++ receives the same program-image byte span as pointer plus size at the native ABI boundary.
TS passes a Uint8Array byte view as one value; C++ receives the same program-symbol byte span as pointer plus size at the native ABI boundary.
TS represents encoded constants as a JavaScript union; C++ stores the same linked-image values in a native variant carrier.
TS aliases ProgramSymbolsImage to imported ProgramMetadata; C++ aliases to the same metadata owner but the parity scanner resolves the included C++ struct fields locally.
TS object-image data bytes are Uint8Array; C++ stores the same bytes in std::vector<u8>.
TS module proto records use object literals; C++ stores the same path/proto pairs as std::pair in the native rodata vector.
TS text section code bytes are Uint8Array; C++ stores the same instruction bytes in std::vector<u8>.
TS keeps const relocation kind as the ROM object-image string token emitted by the compiler; C++ decodes the same token into a scoped native enum at the loader boundary.
TS module proto entries are object records; C++ module proto entries are the same path/proto pairs as native std::pair records.
TS returns the owned Program object directly; C++ returns the same Program owner through unique_ptr at the native ownership boundary.
C++ exposes an additional pointer-plus-length overload for native buffers; TS ArrayLike covers the JS runtime array-like boundary.
TS represents the two-argument and four-argument lower_bound forms with default parameters; C++ exposes the same contract as overloads.

exclude.reason: TS emits OPCODE as a runtime value table for const-enum consumers; C++ exposes the same opcode values through enum class OpCode.

shape_exclude.reason: TS passes a Uint8Array byte view as one value; C++ receives the same ROM metadata byte span as pointer plus size at the native ABI boundary.

exclude.reason: TS tokenKeyFromAsset reads optional TOC token fields from RomAsset; C++ verifies TOC tokens on decode and stores native keys separately.
C++ tokens.h exposes native ROM package map-key aliases; TS owns those asset id aliases in rompack/format.ts.
C++ keeps packed 64-bit token helpers for unordered_map keys; TS public token surface uses lo/hi parts.

C++ keeps packed 64-bit token helpers for unordered_map keys; TS public token surface uses lo/hi parts.

shape_exclude.reason: TS stores public asset tokens as lo/hi words because JavaScript cannot represent the full 64-bit token exactly; C++ owns the same token as native uint64_t and exposes split/make helpers at the ABI edge.
TS returns the public lo/hi AssetToken record; C++ returns the same token lanes as AssetTokenParts for native hashing.
TS render camera view matrix is a number array; C++ stores the same matrix lanes in a fixed native array.
TS render camera projection matrix is a number array; C++ stores the same matrix lanes in a fixed native array.
TS render camera eye vector is a fixed tuple; C++ stores the same three lanes in a fixed native array.
TS uses null for absent camera state; C++ uses std::optional for the same absence contract.
TS glyph submissions allow a single string without allocating a string array; C++ stores the same glyph run payload as a native vector of strings.
TS uses null for absent font at the JS object boundary; C++ stores the same optional font owner as a native pointer.
TS mesh submissions use Float32Array lanes; C++ stores the same matrix lanes in a fixed native array.
TS mesh submissions use Float32Array joint matrices; C++ stores the same matrices in native fixed-array vectors.
TS particle submissions use the vec3arr tuple alias; C++ stores the same three lanes in the native Vec3 carrier.
TS particle submissions use the vec2arr tuple alias; C++ stores the same two lanes in a native fixed array.
TS particle submissions use the vec2arr tuple alias; C++ stores the same two lanes in a native fixed array.
TS narrows particle ambient mode with a literal numeric union; C++ stores the same hardware word as a native integer.
TS polygon points use the rompack Polygon tuple stream alias; C++ stores the same numeric point stream in std::vector<f32>.
TS mesh submissions carry a required Mesh object; C++ stores the same mesh owner as a native pointer in the submission struct.
TS uses fixed tuple arrays for vec2arr; C++ uses std::array with the same two f32 lanes.
TS uses fixed tuple arrays for vec3arr; C++ uses std::array with the same three f32 lanes.
TS resolved camera view matrix is a Float32Array view; C++ exposes the same matrix as a native Mat4 reference.
TS resolved camera projection matrix is a Float32Array view; C++ exposes the same matrix as a native Mat4 reference.
TS resolved camera view-projection matrix is a Float32Array view; C++ exposes the same matrix as a native Mat4 reference.
TS resolved skybox matrix is a Float32Array view; C++ exposes the same matrix as a native Mat4 reference.
TS resolved camera position uses the rompack vec3 alias; C++ exposes the same vector as a native Vec3 reference.
TS receives a typed glyph callback function; C++ keeps the same callback contract as a template parameter to avoid std::function allocation in the hot glyph path.
TS VDP surface pixels are a Uint8Array view; C++ stores the same pixel buffer as a native u8 pointer.
